title: Grafana Alerting Notification Channel Delivery Failures
dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:alerting` contact point dispatch logs, integration errors
app: Grafana unified alerting with debug logging to Splunk
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:alerting"
| search "notification" OR contact_point OR contactPoint
| eval failed=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)failed to send|delivery failed|retry|429|503|invalid webhook"),1,0)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as attempts, sum(failed) as failures by _time, contact_point, integration_type
| eval fail_rate=round(100*failures/nullif(attempts,0),2)
| where failures > 0 AND fail_rate > 1
| sort -failures
description: When Slack webhooks or PagerDuty routing keys break, Grafana fires alerts that never arrive—worse than noisy paging because teams assume silence means health. Channel-level failure rates isolate broken integrations quickly.
value: Restores confidence that Grafana-native alerting paths actually reach on-call tools.
implementation: Normalize `integration_type` (`slack`, `pagerduty`, `teams`). Deduplicate on `fingerprint` if present. Create a dead-letter index for raw payloads with secrets redacted.
visualization: Line chart (fail_rate by channel), pie of integration types failing, table with last error snippet.
grandmaExplanation: We track failed deliveries for alert messages to chat and paging tools. Silence is dangerous if it is because messages never left the building.
